subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 2
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer, type your answer in the box, or fill in the blank to make the equation true. You may not use a calculator.

questions.questionText: The array shows buttons arranged in equal rows. How many buttons are there in all?
questions.explanation: 4 rows of 6 buttons is 4 × 6 = 24.

questions.questionText: What is 7 × 9?
There are 48 crayons shared equally among 6 boxes. How many crayons go in each box?
questions.explanation: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 crayons in each box.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Devon knows that 5 × 7 = 35. Which fact can help him find 6 × 7?
questions.explanation: 6 × 7 is one more group of 7 than 5 × 7, so 35 + 7 = 42. This uses the distributive property: 6 × 7 = (5 × 7) + (1 × 7).

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Riley buys 7 packs of stickers. Each pack has 6 stickers. She gives 8 stickers to her brother. How many stickers does Riley have left?
questions.explanation: First 7 × 6 = 42 stickers. Then 42 − 8 = 34 stickers left. The distractor 42 is the total before giving any away.

questions.questionText: A store has 5 shelves with 8 cans on each shelf. A worker adds 7 more cans. How many cans are there now?
questions.explanation: 5 × 8 = 40 cans, then 40 + 7 = 47 cans.

questions.questionText: Look at the pattern: 6, 12, 18, 24, ___. What number comes next?
questions.explanation: The numbers increase by 6 each time. 24 + 6 = 30.

questions.questionText: Which statement is ALWAYS true?
options: The product of two odd numbers is odd
The sum of two even numbers is odd
The sum of an odd number and an even number is even
questions.explanation: Odd × odd is always odd (e.g., 3 × 5 = 15). Two even numbers add to an even number, multiples of 4 are even, and odd + even is odd.

questions.questionText: Select ALL expressions that are equal to 24.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
A worker pours 24 liters of juice equally into 4 coolers. How many liters of juice are in each cooler?
questions.explanation: 24 ÷ 4 = 6 liters in each cooler.

questions.questionText: What is 458 + 376?
What number do these base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 4 hundreds, 0 tens, and 9 ones make 409.

questions.questionText: What is 649 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: To round to the nearest hundred, look at the tens digit. It is 4, so round DOWN: 649 rounds to 600. Do not be tricked by the 9 in the ones place.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 5 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 5/8.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 4 equal parts. The point is at the third mark, which is 3/4.

questions.questionText: On a number line from 0 to 1, Point A is at 1/6 and Point B is at 1/3. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: When the numerator is 1, the fraction with the SMALLER denominator is larger because the whole is cut into fewer, bigger pieces. Thirds are bigger than sixths, so 1/3 > 1/6, and B is to the right of A.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.explanation: Bar B shows 4 of 8 parts shaded, the same amount as 1 of 2. So 4/8 = 1/2.

questions.questionText: Select ALL comparisons that are TRUE.
questions.explanation: 5/6 > 2/6 (same denominator, bigger numerator), 4/4 = 1 (all parts shaded), and 1/2 > 1/6 (halves are bigger than sixths). But 1/3 < 1/2, so that one is false.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 1 of the 6 equal parts is shaded, so 1/6.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to the whole number 1?
questions.explanation: When all 4 of 4 equal parts are shaded, the whole circle is filled. 4/4 = 1.

questions.questionText: There are 18 grapes on a plate. Sam eats 1/3 of them. How many grapes does Sam eat?
questions.explanation: To find 1/3 of 18, split into 3 equal groups: 18 ÷ 3 = 6 grapes.

questions.questionText: Select ALL fractions that are equal to 1/3.
questions.explanation: 2/6 = 1/3 and 3/9 = 1/3. But 2/3 and 4/6 both equal 2/3, which is more than 1/3.

questions.questionText: Tomas ate 3/4 of a small sandwich. Nina ate 3/8 of a same-size sandwich. Who ate more?
questions.explanation: Both have 3 pieces, but fourths are bigger than eighths, so 3/4 > 3/8. Tomas ate more.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 3 equal parts. The point is at the first mark, which is 1/3.

questions.questionText: There are 20 books on a shelf. One fifth (1/5) of them are red. How many books are red?
questions.explanation: To find 1/5 of 20, divide into 5 equal groups: 20 ÷ 5 = 4 red books.

questions.questionText: What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 7 × 5 = 35 square cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is 10 feet long and 3 feet wide. What is its PERIMETER?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 10 + 3 + 10 + 3 = 26 ft. The distractor 30 is the AREA (10 × 3), a common area-vs-perimeter mix-up.

questions.questionText: A square rug has sides that are each 9 feet long. What is the perimeter of the rug, in feet?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides. Perimeter = 9 + 9 + 9 + 9 = 36 feet.

questions.questionText: Art class starts at 1:50. It lasts 40 minutes. What time does art class end?
questions.explanation: 1:50 + 40 minutes crosses the hour. 1:50 + 10 = 2:00, then 30 more minutes makes 2:30.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is between 8 and 9, and the minute hand points to 9, which is 45 minutes. The time is 8:45.

questions.questionText: Select ALL statements that are TRUE.
options: A pitcher holding 2 liters fills four 2-liter jugs
A 4-kilogram melon is 2 kilograms heavier than a 6-kilogram melon
6 bags of flour, each 3 kilograms, have a mass of 18 kilograms in all
Four 5-liter buckets hold 20 liters in all
questions.explanation: 6 × 3 = 18 kilograms (true) and 4 × 5 = 20 liters (true). One 2-liter pitcher cannot fill four 2-liter jugs (you would need 8 liters), and a 4-kilogram melon is lighter, not heavier, than a 6-kilogram one.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows books read by four students. How many MORE books did Pam read than Leo?
questions.explanation: Pam read 15 books and Leo read 6. 15 − 6 = 9 more books.

questions.questionText: Each ⚽ stands for 4 goals scored. How many MORE goals did the Lions score than the Bears?
questions.explanation: Lions: 6 × 4 = 24 goals. Bears: 2 × 4 = 8 goals. 24 − 8 = 16 more goals. The distractor 4 is the difference in symbols, not goals.

questions.questionText: Select ALL shapes that are quadrilaterals.
questions.explanation: Squares, rhombuses, and trapezoids all have exactly 4 sides, so they are quadrilaterals. A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 7 × 5 = 35, and 35 = 20 + 15, so the blank is 20.
